PO(1).description:
Project summary, including:
- Statement of primary purpose of the project
- Location details, including principal meridian(s), section(s), township(s), and range(s)
- UIC project number, if any
- Current PAL date, if any
- Injection zone name(s)
- Clearly stated method used to define the AOR (e.g., fixed ¼-mile radius, calculated ZEI, or other)
- Project duration
- Volume (including historical, if applicable)
- Number of injection wells
- Presence or absence of freshwater and/or USDW
- A regional location map showing oilfield and other significant boundaries, if any, is recommended to aid reviews
- Map of the AOR showing:
(The historical term 'project area' may be shown as the map view projection of the injection zone defined in 1720.1(g). The 'project area' can include a single AOR, multiple AORs, or any overlapping cumulative effects of the AORs.)
- All wells within and adjacent to the boundary of the AOR, including surface and bottom-hole locations, wellbore paths, well identifiers, and symbols which display their well type and status (e.g. active steamflood injector)
- All water supply wells that are within the AOR and identified in public records or known to operator
- All wells which have been identified as potential conduits to a USDW
- Any underground disposal horizons, mining, and other subsurface industrial activities not associated with oil and gas production within the AOR, to the extent such information is publicly available
- Traces of the geologic cross-sections provided as part of the Geologic Study
- All faults within the project area

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for PO(1).

CL(1).description:
Cover letter including a statement that appropriate California-licensed professional(s) are responsible for all data, interpretations, and calculations,  if any, in accordance with Business and Professions Code sections (BPC) 6730-6749, 7835 and, 7835.1 as applicable. Include the following:
- A statement with supporting rationale identifying whether injected Class II fluids have migrated beyond the exempt aquifer or into a USDW for ongoing projects, and demonstrating that such migration will not occur for any project.
- Stamp and signature of appropriate licensed professional(s) at bottom of page.
- If the operator determines that the submission does not include data, interpretations, or calculations subject to the requirements of BPC sections 7835 and 7835.1, the cover page must so indicate, and must provide the name(s) and signature(s) of the individual(s) responsible for preparing the submission.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for CL(1).

DF(1).description: All data supporting the underground injection project are in digital format.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for DF(1).

DF(2).description:
Maps are clearly and appropriately labeled with:
- Title
- Scale
- North arrow
- UIC project number, if any
- Legend describing symbols, colors, and other key features shown on map
- Well symbols and well identifiers are clearly posted on each map
- Significant Boundaries, including the area of review, project area, and existing exemption boundaries
- Other relevant data
- Diagrams and exhibits are clearly and appropriately labeled.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for DF(2).

GS(1).description:
The scope of the geologic characterization needs to encompass:
- Stratigraphy of project area
- Injection Zone
- Sealing mechanism
- Upper confining layer (caprock)
- Lower confining layer, if any
- Areas/zones of potential migration or entrapment of fluid
- Discussion of any identified geologic features that could result in the migration of fluid out of the approved injection zone
- All supporting data which is most current, accurate, and relevant
- Method and data used to determine the base of the USDW
- All data sources cited
- Geologic information describing the reservoir characteristics of the injection zone, such as:
- Porosity (min, max, average)
- Permeability (min, max, average)
- Thickness (min, max, average)
- Fracture gradient
- Original temperature
- Current temperature
- Original pressure
- Current pressure
- Original oil saturation
- Residual oil saturation
- Original gas saturation
- Residual gas saturation
- Original water saturation
- Residual water saturation

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(1).

GS(2).description:
Reservoir fluid data for each injection zone, such as:
- Oil gravity
- Viscosity
- Presence and concentration of non-hydrocarbon components in associated gas (such as hydrogen sulfide)
- Specific gravity of gas
- Water quality data and analysis of reservoir fluid, in accordance with Section 1724.7.2, including the following:
- TDS determination (including, but not limited to, historical data, log derivations, constants for verification)
The Division and Water Boards agree TDS documentation needs be based on actual samples when available.
- Total petroleum hydrocarbon as crude oil; major cations (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Fe, Mn, Sr, B); major anions (CI, SO4, HCO3, CO3, Br, I); total alkalinity and hydroxide; electrical conductance; pH; and temperature.
- Injection zone groundwater analysis should be sampled from either the injection zone itself prior to commencement of any injection or sampled from an analogous reservoir which has not already received injection fluid, and sample(s) should be:
- Representative of the reservoir groundwater in its native condition
- Recovered after all completion and drilling fluid has been circulated from the wellbore
- Sample collected within past 5 years, for new and expansion UIC applications
- Any proposed groundwater sampling and analysis proposed for new or modified projects needs to follow the most recent version of the Notice to Operators - Guidelines for Collection of Oilfield Water Quality Data

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(2).

GS(3).description:
Representative electric type log which:
- Extends to a depth below deepest producing or injection zone, whichever is deeper
- Identifies geophysical curves
- Includes a vertical scale
- Identifies the API number
- Identifies all geologic units/formations
- Identifies location of approved, or proposed, injection zone(s) and confining zone(s)
- Identifies freshwater aquifers and the base of freshwater (defined in §1720.1(e) as water containing TDS ≤3000 mg/L, regardless of hydrocarbon content or exemption status)
- Identifies USDWs and the base of USDW
- Identifies any oil and/or gas zones

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(3).

GS(4).description:
Structural contour map, indicating any faults and lateral containment features of the following:
(For multiple distinct injection zones and confining units, structural maps for each zone are required)
- Top of upper confining layer/s, if any
- Base of upper confining layer/s, if any (if different than the top of injection zone)
- Top of Injection zone/s
- Base of injection zone/s (if different than top of lower confining layer)
- Top of lower confining layer/s, if any
- Base of lower confining layer/s, if any
- Each map is accurate, follows items in DF(2), and includes the following:
- Name of the formation or unit mapped
- Lines of cross-sections
- All wells with adequate data across mapped zone need to be incorporated into contouring and identified on maps
- Elevation tops for each well for which elevation can be determined are clearly posted on each map
- Wellbore paths shown for deviated/horizontal wells, if any (zone tops should be shown at location of wellbore penetration)
- An additional higher-resolution map may be submitted where labels become illegible due to high well density.
- Faults identified in area (with displacement information).
- If faults are identified, an analysis addressing whether or not the faults are capable of confining the fluid to the approved injection zone (e.g., pressure data, water quality data, juxtaposition analysis, or shale gouge ratio for clay smear analysis)

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(4).

GS(5).description:
Isopach contour maps drawn for each of the following zones (for multiple distinct injection zones and confining units, isopach maps for each zone are required):
- Injection zone/s (net)
- Upper confining layer/s, if any
- Lower confining layer/s, if any
- Each map is accurate, follows items in DF(2), and includes the following:
- Name of the formation or unit mapped
- Lines of cross-sections
- All wells which penetrate the top of the mapped zone/layer should be shown on map. If contours are based on calculations for individual wells, values for each well should be provided on map (if contours are generated from surface-to-surface gridding in mapping software, this is to be indicated).
- Well symbols, well identifiers, and data values used for contouring are clearly posted on each map 
(An additional higher-resolution map may be submitted where labels become illegible due to high well density)
- Wellbore paths shown for deviated/horizontal wells, if any

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(5).

GS(6).description:
At least two geologic cross-sections in the area of review. As near as possible, one cross-section should be along strike and the other should be perpendicular to strike.
- Each cross-section needs to:
- Include a legend describing symbols, colors, and other key features shown on cross-sections
- Extend from base of deepest production or injection zone to surface
- Include at least 3 wells and include API number(s)
- Include at least 1 injection well and include API number(s)
- Include deviated wells within the line of section, if any (i.e., wells near the cross-section trace)
- Include water supply wells, if any
- Have vertical depth (in SubSea TVD) and horizontal scale clearly labeled
- Have datum flattened on sea-level
- Indicate the formations or units penetrated by injection wells
- Identify faults and indicate displacement
- Indicate location of each approved, or proposed, injection zone(s)
- Indicate location of confining units
- Indicate freshwater zones and base of freshwater
- Indicate USDW zones and base of USDW
- Indicate AOR extents
- Indicate the extents of aquifer exemption boundaries where applicable

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(6).

GS(7).description:
If the injection zone is not an exempted aquifer, then a non-USDW determination needs to be made using recent (collected within the past 5 years) water quality samples of the injection zone groundwater within the project area. If no wells exist within the project area, then the total dissolved solids (TDS) well log calculations can be used provided that: 1) the accuracy (i.e., percent error) of the computed result is validated with a water quality sample of the same injection zone groundwater within one mile of the project area; and 2) the method, calculations, and assumptions used in the TDS analysis are provided (including available lab and sampling reports).

If the operator determines through water quality sampling or TDS calculations that the injection zone and/or overlying formations are non-USDWs, the lateral and vertical extent of this determination needs to be defined on the application maps and cross-sections.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(7).

GS(8).description: Any groundwater quality data or well log analysis that will be used to justify the injection of fluids (e.g., showing that TDS concentrations within the proposed injection zone are greater than 10,000 milligrams per liter) must be accompanied by a written statement or report, prepared by a state-registered professional geologist or professional engineer, indicating the degree to which the water quality data is representative of the injectate fluid or native formation groundwater and provide justification for that conclusion.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for GS(8).

ES(1).description:
Provide the rationale for determining the AOR. The Division may use the rates, pressures, and volumes included in the calculations as conditions of the Project Approval Letter. For water disposal projects, determination of injectate front and pressure front may be an important part of the area of review determination.
Include:
- Calculations and variables, including values used to determine average porosity, permeability, and thickness (net and gross)
- Citations and assumptions
- Anticipated project duration, anticipated daily rate of injection (by well), and anticipated cumulative volume of fluid to be injected
- Using recent (measured within the past 2 years) idle well fluid level data for all idle wells within the AOR, provide an isobar map showing the apparent groundwater flow direction(s) within the injection zone. The supporting data (e.g., acoustic water level surveys) used to create the maps needs to be provided.
- Provide an evaluation of any potential increase in reservoir pressure and fluid migration due to either overlapping ZEIs, neighboring injection projects, or other relevant inputs into the system (i.e., faults, facies changes and stratigraphic pinch-outs which may act as flow barriers).

If the change in reservoir pressure due to injections will be reduced by production wells, the Operator will submit calculations that show how the change in reservoir pressure will be offset by the production wells. Injection and production wells used in this evaluation need to be identified on the application maps/figures.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for ES(1).

ES(2).description:
For all projects, AOR determinations must use an accurate representation of all the relevant parameters from within the project area, including the static pressures or current temperatures of the injection zone(s) and USDW(s) within the AOR. If a range of values for the input parameters is provided, then multiple AORs need to be determined to provide the most conservative estimate of the distance injected fluids may migrate. This procedure will provide assurance that, if proposed, an AOR of one quarter-mile fixed radius is adequate. A calculation may meet the regulatory requirements of AOR determination. The analysis should consider the heterogeneity of the reservoir.
- If the AOR is determined using a calculation, the chosen method should be supported technically

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for ES(2).

ES(3).description:
A compendium of the following information:
- API Numbers or other identifying information for wells depicted in AOR map which do not have API's (including sidetracks)
- Wellbore Paths (vertical, directional, horizontal)
- Total Depths
- Depths of Completion Interval(s) of the wells
- Documentation showing all steps of cement yield and cement calculations performed.
- Well Construction diagrams for all wells and boreholes within the AOR which penetrate the injection zone need to be provided. Each diagram should identify all of the data specified in Section 1724.7.1, including but limited to:
- Depth
- Perforated/Slotted Interval(s)
- Cement (e.g., annular seal, plugs, and squeezes)
- Casing Damage
- Key Geologic Markers (e.g. injection zone, confining zone, all oil and gas zones)
- Base of Freshwater
- Base of USDW
- Each well and borehole diagram needs to depict the entire history (e.g. sidetracks, redrills, and other mechanical changes)

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for ES(3).

ES(4).description:
The planned well-drilling program.
The Division recognizes plans are subject to change during the course of a project review. It is the operator's responsibility to update project data accordingly prior to approval.
- Planned plugging-and-abandonment program (include any applicable idle well management plan identification number).
- Flood pattern map (if applicable) showing:
- All injection, production, and plugged and abandoned wells
- Unit boundaries

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for ES(4).

ES(5).description:
Provide a water well survey within one mile of the AOR. On a case-by-case basis, an expanded water well survey may be necessary based upon potential risk to beneficial use water outside the limits of the AOR. Water well locations need to be presented on a map and listed in an accompanying table which includes the following information:
- Location information (For well completion reports that do not provide the latitude and longitude coordinates, the Operator should attempt to determine its location by a field survey)
- Type (municipal, domestic, irrigation, industrial, stock)
- Status (active, idle, abandoned, destroyed, unknown)
- Owner
- Well completion depth and zone name
- Depths for all screened intervals
- The water well survey should utilize the following data sources (at a minimum):
- Department of Water Resources (DWR) well completion reports and Water Data Library Station Map
- GeoTracker Groundwater Ambient Monitoring and Assessment (GAMA) information system

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for ES(5).

IP(1).description:
Anticipated project duration.
- Anticipated daily rate of injection by well - based on maximum values.
- Anticipated cumulative net volume of fluid to be injected - based on maximum values.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(1).

IP(2).description:
Identification of all wells that are part of the project, including:
- Injection wells
- Affected production wells
- Water source wells and their depths
- Observation wells and/or other wells
- Planned wells (to the extent known)

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(2).

IP(3).description:
Provide representative water quality data of injectate, collected within past year, including:
- Total petroleum hydrocarbon as crude oil; major cations (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Fe, Mn, Sr, B); major anions (CI, SO4, HCO3, CO3, Br, I); total alkalinity and hydroxide; electrical conductance; pH; and temperature.
- Identification of the source(s) of injected fluids, including zone/formation and approximate volume percentages
- Analyses of the injectate in accordance with Section 1724.7.2, including:
- Sampling after all additives (if any) are added to the liquid and after all treatment or separation process (if any), to ensure that it is representative of the liquid actually injected. 
Sampling protocol for existing projects (project-by-project) should be provided.
Any proposed water sampling and analysis proposed for new or modified projects and should follow the most recent version of the Notice to Operators - Guidelines for Collection of Oilfield Water Quality Data.
- Provide the type of treatment and plan for the disposal of reject water.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(3).

IP(4).description:
A description of method of injection including such information as:
- Injection string configuration

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(4).

IP(5).description: A list of the cathodic protection or other corrosion prevention measures employed for plants, lines, and wells, if such measures are warranted.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(5).

IP(6).description:
Monitoring system including methods or standard operating procedures to be utilized to ensure no damage is occurring and that the injection fluids are confined to approved injection zone.
In the event the Division or Water Boards require groundwater monitoring in relation to the UIC, or as a condition of project approval, the operator should consult with the State Water Resources Control Board or the Regional Water Quality Control Board and provide the Division with documentation and the results of such consultation.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(6).

IP(7).description:
The Aquifer Exemption (AE) conditions as outlined in State Water Board's final concurrence letters need to be addressed in the Operator's application and incorporated into the Project Approval Letters, if appropriate. Such AE conditions may include monitoring that is different than the monitoring requested at the UIC project level. Requested "groundwater monitoring" includes but is not limited to the following: water quality monitoring (e.g., of the injectate and/or groundwater within the newly exempted zone/area), temperature monitoring, and/or pressure monitoring.
- For those projects with potential conduits into a USDW, a proposed remediation and/or monitoring plan to confirm the pressure front calculations used in determining the change in pressure near problem wells or faults needs to be provided.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for IP(7).

SI(1).description:
Any other data that, in the judgement of the Division, are pertinent and necessary for the proper evaluation of a UIC project. Examples may include:
- Health protection zone evaluation
- Isochore maps
- Isogor maps
- Isobar maps (e.g., base of USDW, overlying hydrocarbon zones)
- Water-oil ratio maps
- 3D geologic models
- Reservoir simulation results
- Isopach maps of the confining layers
- Reservoir temperature maps
- Discussion on Lawrence Livermore National Labs (LLNL) HPSC Risk Factors
- Equipment diagrams
- Safety programs
- Surface Expression Monitoring and Prevention Plan (SEMPP)
- Map of surface expressions, cracks, fissures, sink holes (projects with known surface expressions and steam injection into diatomaceous zone, unless no concern proven).

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for SI(1).

SI(2).description: Any alternative data accepted by the Division in lieu of the default regulatory requirements for project data, as described in Section 1724.7(e).

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for SI(2).

SI(3).description:
For a UIC project which includes an injection well with open perforations located within 500 linear feet of the screen or perforations of a water supply well (or a project otherwise required to provide this information as determined by the Division), all of the following information, updated annually:
- A water treatment process flow diagram depicting all physical and chemical treatment processes applied to the injection fluid, from its source to the injection well
- The safety data sheet for each chemical additive emplaced in injection wells within the UIC project, and for each chemical added to the fluid to be injected from the time the fluid is first obtained to the time it is injected
- The project-aggregate volume or weight of each additive reported
- A brief description of the intended purpose of each additive reported

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for SI(3).

OI(1).description:
Data supporting the determination of MASIP for each injection well in the UIC project, as described in 1724.10.3 including:
- All calculations, variables, citations, and assumptions

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for OI(1).

OI(2).description: Copies of letters of notification sent to offset operators by the operator of the UIC project.

projects.Dummy Project: This is a dummy response for OI(2).
